title: A 2-year retrospective cohort study of pulp-related outcomes of permanent anterior teeth with uncomplicated crown fractures.
abstract: Uncomplicated crown fractures (UCFs) are the most common form of traumatic dental injury in permanent dentition. However, studies on long-term pulp outcome in South Asian populations are limited. This study aimed to analyse pulp survival of permanent anterior teeth with uncomplicated crown fractures in a Sri Lankan population.
